sourceMovie.title: Apocalypse Now
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Apocalypse Now for the same reason it broke through in 1979: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a war rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Francis Ford Coppola keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and war blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Full Metal Jacket
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Apocalypse Now clicked for you, Full Metal Jacket (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and war, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Apocalypse Now?
faq.answer: Try Full Metal Jacket, Platoon, 12 Strong—each shares DNA with Apocalypse Now while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Apocalypse Now?
faq.answer: Apocalypse Now (1979) is best described as Drama, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Apocalypse Now worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
